topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed a monotonous tune, and Leo found his eyelids growing heavy. He stared blankly at the application form, the ink blurring slightly under his gaze. He reread the section on required experience, his focus drifting to the ceiling tiles. A wave of profound weariness washed over him, making the chair feel suddenly too soft. He sighed, a long, drawn-out exhalation that barely disturbed the air around him. He wondered if he had actually read the entire description, or if his brain was just refusing to engage any further. He picked up his pen, the movement sluggish, and considered the sheer effort of articulating his qualifications when his limbs felt weighted with inertia.

[story 2]

Maya leaned back in her worn desk chair, her head tilting precariously to the side. The afternoon sun, streaming through the blinds, cast stripes of warm light across the documents. Her eyes kept fluttering, struggling to maintain concentration on the resume she was meticulously proofreading. Every word seemed to swim together in a viscous haze. She rubbed her temples with the heels of her hands, a small, involuntary gesture of strain. "This is ridiculous," she mumbled to herself, the sound muffled by the quiet office. The prospect of finishing this task felt monumental, like pushing a heavy boulder uphill when one was already running on fumes. She blinked slowly, trying to force her vision into sharper focus, but the edges of the page kept softening.

[story 3]

Ethan slumped forward, resting his chin heavily on his crossed arms. The air in the room felt thick and slow, making every breath feel like a deliberate effort. He glanced at the identical job posting displayed on his screen, a familiar sense of impending shutdown creeping into his awareness. His fingers hovered over the keyboard, hesitant and clumsy. He could feel a distinct heaviness settling in his limbs, a compelling urge to simply let the tension drain away. He let his gaze drift toward the window, watching a distant bird, finding the movement mesmerizingly slow. He wanted nothing more than to close his eyes and let the world fade into a gentle, undisturbed quiet.
